---
title: "DATA3888 Volatility Modelling Report"
author: "Finance 6"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

> Version: aligned with the latest backend and Streamlit workflow ,reads backend artifacts and report-ready figures
> Need to complete:1."To be continue". 2.change the plot bar color

```{python}
#| label: setup

from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import display, Markdown, Image

ROOT = Path(".")
ARTIFACT_RUNS = ROOT / "artifacts" / "runs"
REPORT_FIGURES = ROOT / "plots" / "report_figures"
DASHBOARD_FIGURES = ROOT / "figures"


def md(text: str):
    display(Markdown(text))


def placeholder(msg: str):
    md(f"> **Placeholder:** {msg}")


def latest_run_dir():
    if not ARTIFACT_RUNS.exists():
        return None
    runs = sorted([p for p in ARTIFACT_RUNS.iterdir() if p.is_dir()], reverse=True)
    return runs[0] if runs else None


RUN_DIR = latest_run_dir()


def load_artifact(name: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Load a parquet artifact from the latest backend run."""
    if RUN_DIR is None:
        placeholder("No backend run artifacts were found. Run the backend pipeline from Streamlit first.")
        return pd.DataFrame()
    path = RUN_DIR / name
    if not path.exists():
        placeholder(f"Missing artifact `{name}` in `{RUN_DIR.as_posix()}`.")
        return pd.DataFrame()
    try:
        return pd.read_parquet(path)
    except Exception as exc:
        placeholder(f"Could not read `{path.as_posix()}`: {exc}")
        return pd.DataFrame()


def show_png(path: str, width: int = 900):
    """Display one static PNG if it exists; otherwise show a clear placeholder."""
    img = ROOT / path
    if not img.exists():
        placeholder(f"Missing figure `{path}`. Need a dashboard screenshot.")
        return
    display(Image(filename=str(img), width=width))


def show_table(df: pd.DataFrame, n: int | None = None):
    if df.empty:
        placeholder("Table unavailable because the relevant backend output is missing or empty.")
        return
    display(df if n is None else df.head(n))


def artifact_status_table():
    expected = [
        "config.json",
        "status.json",
        "predictions.parquet",
        "metrics.parquet",
        "feature_importance.parquet",
        "universe_summary.parquet",
        "stock_similarity.parquet",
    ]
    rows = []
    for name in expected:
        path = RUN_DIR / name if RUN_DIR else None
        rows.append({
            "name": name,
            "path": path.as_posix() if path else "No run directory found",
            "exists": bool(path and path.exists()),
            "size_bytes": path.stat().st_size if path and path.exists() else None,
        })
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


metrics = load_artifact("metrics.parquet")
predictions = load_artifact("predictions.parquet")
feature_importance = load_artifact("feature_importance.parquet")
universe_summary = load_artifact("universe_summary.parquet")
similarity = load_artifact("stock_similarity.parquet")

if RUN_DIR is not None:
    md(f"Using backend run artifacts from `{RUN_DIR.as_posix()}`.")
```

```{python}
#| label: generate-report-figures

import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from IPython.display import display, Markdown

PROJECT_ROOT = Path.cwd()
script_path = PROJECT_ROOT / "back_end" / "generate_report_figures.py"

if not script_path.exists():
    display(Markdown("> **Placeholder:** `back_end/generate_report_figures.py` was not found. Static report figures were not regenerated."))
else:
    result = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, str(script_path)],
        cwd=str(PROJECT_ROOT),
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )
    if result.stdout:
        print(result.stdout)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        if result.stderr:
            print(result.stderr)
        display(Markdown("> **Placeholder:** Report figure generation did not complete successfully. Existing figures, if present, will still be used below."))
```


## Introduction

The financial market changes rapidly. Short-term stock market fluctuations play an important role in trading, risk management, and other financial decisions. Volatility forecasting estimates the likely size of future price movements. More accurate forecasts can help market participants manage risk and make more informed trading decisions.

This project focuses on short-term realised volatility prediction using Optiver limit order book data. The task is to use information from the first part of a trading window to predict realised volatility over a future horizon. The current project question is:

> **What is the best model given latency constraints, for a single stock or multiple stocks?**

This means that the project does not only compare models by accuracy....
> To be continue---

## Problem Context and Research Question

> To be continue---research question: framed around model selection under latency constraints

## Data and Feature Engineering

> To be continue---

## Modelling Workflow

Raw parquet order book data
        ↓
Feature engineering and feature cache
        ↓
Model configuration in Streamlit
        ↓
Backend model run
        ↓
Saved artifacts in artifacts/runs/<run_id>/
        ↓
Dashboard visualisation ＆ Quarto report figures

> To be continue---

## Forecasting Models

> To be continue---summarise model families used:linear models.........

## Evaluation Framework

> To be continue---the evaluation metrics used for comparison(rmse,qlike)

```{python}
#| label: metrics-overview

if metrics.empty:
    placeholder("Metrics artifact unavailable. Run the backend pipeline first.")
else:
    cols = [c for c in ["model", "fold", "rmse", "mae", "qlike", "mean_inference_ms", "inference_ms"] if c in metrics.columns]
    if cols:
        show_table(metrics[cols].head(15))
    else:
        show_table(metrics.head(15))
```

## Results

### Accuracy--Latency Trade-off

This section answers the question, **What is the best model given latency constraints, for a single stock or multiple stocks?**

Compare model inference time and accuracy; therefore, models closer to the bottom right corner are faster and more accurate.

```{python}
#| label: fig-accuracy-latency

show_png("plots/report_figures/accuracy_latency_tradeoff.png")
```

### Model Metric Comparison

The model comparison chart includes accuracy metrics such as root mean square error(RMSE) and QLIKE.

These metrics allow for comparison of model performance from both general regression and volatility perspectives.

```{python}
#| label: fig-model-metrics

show_png("plots/report_figures/model_metric_comparison.png")
```

> To be continue---Figure.........

```{python}
#| label: model-metrics-table

if metrics.empty:
    placeholder("Model metric table unavailable.")
else:
    overall = metrics[metrics["fold"] == 0] if "fold" in metrics.columns else metrics
    keep = [c for c in ["model", "rmse", "mae", "qlike", "mape", "rmspe", "pearson_r"] if c in overall.columns]
    if keep:
        show_table(overall[keep].sort_values(keep[1]) if len(keep) > 1 else overall[keep])
    else:
        show_table(overall.head(20))
```

### Single-Stock Model Selection

The individual-stock analysis allows users to inspect how models behave for a selected stock and time window. This is important because the best model for one stock may not be the best model for all stocks.

```{python}
#| label: fig-single-stock

show_png("plots/report_figures/single_stock_actual_vs_predicted.png")
```

> To be continue---Figure.........

### Universe-Level Model Selection

The universe-level analysis compares model behaviour across multiple stocks. This helps answer whether one model performs well generally, or whether different stocks require different models.

```{python}
#| label: fig-universe-wins

show_png("plots/report_figures/universe_best_model_counts.png")
```

```{python}
#| label: universe-summary-table

if universe_summary.empty:
    placeholder("Universe summary artifact unavailable.")
else:
    keep = [c for c in ["stock_id", "mean_volatility", "rmse", "mae", "qlike", "best_model"] if c in universe_summary.columns]
    if keep:
        show_table(universe_summary[keep].head(20))
    else:
        show_table(universe_summary.head(20))
```

### Feature Importance (perhap this section should be moved forward?)

Feature importance helps explain which variables contribute most to model predictions. This supports interpretability and helps connect model behaviour back to the order book features.

```{python}
#| label: fig-feature-importance

show_png("plots/report_figures/feature_importance_top10.png")
```

## Dashboard and Visualisation Interface

> To be continue---

### Model Specification Page

```{python}
#| label: dashboard-model-spec-screenshot

show_png("figures/dashboard_model_specification.png")
```

> To be continue---

### Individual Stock Page

```{python}
#| label: dashboard-individual-screenshot

show_png("figures/dashboard_individual_stock.png")
```

> To be continue---

### Universe Page

```{python}
#| label: dashboard-universe-screenshot

show_png("figures/dashboard_universe.png")
```

> To be continue---

## Limitations(?)

> To be continue---

## Future Work(?)

> To be continue---

## Conclusion

> To be continue---

## Appendix

```{python}
#| label: appendix-artifact-audit

md("### Backend artifact availability")
show_table(artifact_status_table())
```
